title: Emergency Guide: Cat Dragging Back Legs and Vocalizing in Pain
meta_desc: If your cat is dragging their back legs and in pain, they need immediate emergency care. Learn about Saddle Thrombus and why every second counts.
content_html:
What is it?

When a cat suddenly loses the use of their back legs and is in visible distress, it is most often caused by a Feline Aortic Thromboembolism (FATE), commonly referred to as a 'Saddle Thrombus.' This condition occurs when a blood clot, often originating from the heart, travels through the aorta and becomes lodged at the junction where the blood supply splits to the hind legs. This blockages cuts off circulation, leading to sudden paralysis and intense pain.

Is it an emergency?

Yes. This is a critical medical emergency with a high urgency level. If your cat is experiencing these symptoms, you should not wait. Seek immediate veterinary care at an emergency 24-hour clinic. This condition is life-threatening and requires professional intervention to manage pain and attempt to restore blood flow.

Why a photo or video helps triage

If it is safe to do so without delaying your departure, taking a quick 5-second video of your cat's movement or a clear photo of their paw pads can be incredibly helpful. The veterinary team will look for signs such as 'cyanosis' (a blue or pale tint to the paw pads) and the specific way the limbs are being held to confirm a diagnosis and prioritize your cat for immediate stabilization upon arrival.

faqs.question: Can I give my cat human pain medication?
faqs.answer: No. Never give your cat human medications like Tylenol or Aspirin, as these are highly toxic and can be fatal. Only a veterinarian can safely administer the necessary pain relief for this condition.

faqs.question: Are there other causes for back leg paralysis?
faqs.answer: While Saddle Thrombus is the most common cause of sudden hind limb paralysis in cats, other possibilities include spinal trauma or severe neurological issues. Regardless of the cause, sudden paralysis is always an emergency.

faqs.question: What should I do while transporting my cat?
faqs.answer: Keep your cat as calm as possible in a secure carrier lined with soft blankets. Do not attempt to massage the legs, as this can be extremely painful for the cat.
